top_categories.workout_context: 621 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
621 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 621 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Utrecht Ultra's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 19,685ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
Utrecht Ultra's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
19,685ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
At Utrecht Ultra, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Utrecht Ultra's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 621 miles.
Tempo is Utrecht Ultra's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 621 miles on mixed terrain. Train it until it's automatic.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Utrecht Ultra. Train it until it feels boring.
19,685ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 621 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 621 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, HVLI Extended, and Endurance to prepare you for 621 miles of mixed terrain in Utrecht, Netherlands.
